question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted source configuration entry via externally reachable configuration endpoints such that a rogue S3StreamFileProvider is instantiated pointing to an attacker-controlled bucket, causing the importer to ingest stream files with tampered transaction fees outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of URI validation in s3Client() when sourceProperties.getUri() is null and sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint() is used, by poisoning the SourceType endpoint resolution to redirect S3 traffic to an attacker-controlled server that serves stream files with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a DNS rebinding attack against the URI resolved by URI.create(sourceProperties.getType().getEndpoint()) in s3Client(), redirecting S3 requests mid-session to an attacker-controlled host that returns stream files with fraudulent transaction fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AnonymousCredentialsProvider.create() path in awsCredentialsProvider() when isAnonymousCredentials() returns true, by placing publicly writable objects in the configured bucket that contain stream files with altered transaction fees, which the importer then downloads without authentication checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StaticCredentialsProvider path in awsCredentialsProvider() by leaking or brute-forcing the AwsBasicCredentials (accessKey/secretKey) from credentials.getAccessKey()/credentials.getSecretKey(), then uploading tampered stream files with modified transaction fees to the configured bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DefaultCredentialsProvider.builder().build() fallback in awsCredentialsProvider() by injecting environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) pointing to attacker-controlled credentials, causing the importer to authenticate to a rogue S3 endpoint and download stream files with tampered transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious projectId value via the StreamSourceProperties.getProjectId() call in overrideConfiguration(), causing the ExecutionInterceptor to append a crafted userProject query parameter that redirects GCP billing or routing to an attacker-controlled project serving tampered stream files with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of sanitization in appendRawQueryParameter(\

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the forcePathStyle(true) setting in s3Client() combined with a manipulated bucket name in commonDownloaderProperties.getBucketName() to redirect path-style S3 requests to an attacker-controlled server hosting stream files with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endpointOverride(sourceProperties.getUri()) call in s3Client() by manipulating the URI stored in StreamSourceProperties to point to an SSRF-reachable internal service that proxies requests to an attacker-controlled bucket containing stream files with tampered transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the iteration over commonDownloaderProperties.getSources() in streamFileProviders() by injecting an additional source entry pointing to an attacker-controlled S3 endpoint, causing the importer to process stream files with modified transaction fees from that rogue source alongside legitimate ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.info() call in streamFileProviders() that logs commonDownloaderProperties.getBucketName() and blockProperties.getBucketName() to extract bucket names via log injection or log exfiltration, then use those bucket names to stage a bucket takeover attack that serves stream files with altered transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the LOCAL source type branch in the switch statement of streamFileProviders() by placing crafted stream files in the local filesystem path used by LocalStreamFileProvider, causing the importer to process transaction fee records that deviate from design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NettyNioAsyncHttpClient configuration in s3Client() where connectionTimeout is sourced from sourceProperties.getConnectionTimeout() with a minimum of 1 second, by setting an extremely short timeout to cause partial stream file downloads that result in incomplete or corrupted transaction fee records being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxConcurrency(sourceProperties.getMaxConcurrency()) setting in s3Client() where the default is 1000, by triggering a high-concurrency race condition during stream file downloads that causes out-of-order processing of transaction fee records, resulting in fees being recorded outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GCP-specific static credentials path in isStaticCredentials() (which returns true when type == SourceType.GCP regardless of credentials being set) to force the importer to use attacker-supplied GCP credentials that authenticate to a rogue GCS bucket serving stream files with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Region.of(sourceProperties.getRegion()) call in s3Client() by injecting a crafted region string that resolves to an attacker-controlled regional endpoint, causing the importer to download stream files with tampered transaction fees from that endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StringUtils.isNotBlank(projectId) check in overrideConfiguration() being the only guard before injecting the projectId into HTTP requests, by supplying a projectId containing HTTP header injection sequences that alter the S3 request to fetch stream files with modified transaction fees from an unintended location?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathPrefix logging in streamFileProviders() (when commonDownloaderProperties.getPathPrefix() is not blank) to infer the storage layout and craft a path traversal attack that causes the importer to read stream files with modified transaction fees from an unintended path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a man-in-the-middle attack against the S3AsyncClient built in s3Client() by exploiting the absence of TLS certificate pinning on the endpointOverride URI, intercepting stream file downloads and substituting files with modified transaction fees before they reach the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: awsCredentialsProvider()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DefaultCredentialsProvider chain in awsCredentialsProvider() by placing a crafted ~/.aws/credentials file or AWS_PROFILE environment variable on the importer host (via a prior low-privilege compromise) to redirect S3 authentication to an attacker-controlled bucket with tampered transaction fee stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFileProviders() creates a new S3AsyncClient per source via s3Client(source) without validating the source URI scheme, by injecting a file:// or ftp:// URI as the endpoint to cause the importer to read stream files with modified transaction fees from an attacker-controlled local or network path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: s3Client()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the connectionMaxIdleTime hardcoded to Duration.ofSeconds(5) in s3Client() to force connection recycling at a predictable interval, enabling a timing attack where the attacker hijacks a recycled connection to inject stream files with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: overrideConfiguration()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the anonymous inner ExecutionInterceptor class in overrideConfiguration() that captures projectId from the outer scope, by causing the projectId to be updated concurrently (if the bean is not truly immutable) to inject a different project ID mid-request, redirecting stream file fetches to a project serving tampered transaction fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/config/CloudStorageConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: streamFileProviders()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the switch expression over source.getType() in streamFileProviders() by supplying a source with type GCP that maps to the S3StreamFileProvider path, causing the importer to use GCP-specific credential logic against an S3-compatible attacker-controlled endpoint that serves stream files with modified transaction fees?
